metadata.countable_import_rule: accepted mismatch-export rows must be explicitly expert_reviewed and carry a non-needs_more_evidence reaction/substrate resolution before import-countable-label-review can count them
metadata.expert_review_signoff_method: research-director desk review (vivek), informed by Claude-Opus-4-7 mechanism analysis of M-CSA curated names/EC/mechanism text and structure context. Per-row rationales recorded on each decision block.
metadata.policy: Automation-curated batch decisions stay bronze and are imported as automation_curated or needs_expert_review records, not gold or expert-reviewed labels.
metadata.review_rule: keyword-level kinase or ATP phosphoryl-transfer mismatch is not enough to create a new ontology family or count a label; export every mismatch lane for expert reaction/substrate review

current_label.rationale: Phosphorylase kinase performs ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer outside the current seed fingerprints.

decision.rationale: phosphorylase kinase (EC 2.7.11.19): ePK-fold Ser/Thr protein kinase, ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer. Pocket Mg2+ is ATP cofactor, not catalytic hydrolytic metal. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: ePK.

mechanism_text_snippets: Asp149 deprotonates the protein substrate hydroxyl group, initiating nucleophilic attack upon the gamma-phosphate of ATP in a substitution reaction that eliminates ADP. The phosphorylated protein deprotonates Asp149 [PMID:10545198].
mismatch_context.review_policy: route both labeled and unlabeled reaction/substrate mismatch lanes to expert review before adding an ontology-family rule or accepting more countable labels

review_items.review_question: Does this entry represent kinase, ATP phosphoryl-transfer, or another non-hydrolytic reaction class that should remain out of scope, use an existing seed fingerprint, or require a new ontology family before any countable label is accepted?

current_label.rationale: Phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase couples decarboxylation to ATP phosphoryl transfer rather than the current hydrolase or redox seed mechanisms.

decision.rationale: PEPCK-ATP (EC 4.1.1.49): carboxy-lyase coupled to ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer; uses Mn2+/Mg2+. Not a hydrolase. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: PEPCK.

mechanism_text_snippets: The substrate undergoes decarboxylation, resulting in the formation of an enol intermediate, stabilised by positively charged residues. The oxyanion formed attacks the gamma phosphate of ATP in a nucleophilic substitution, giving the products of ADP and phosphoenolpyruvate.

current_label.rationale: 2-amino-4-hydroxy-6-hydroxymethyldihydropteridine diphosphokinase performs ATP-dependent pyrophosphoryl transfer, not the current metal-hydrolase seed.

decision.rationale: HPPK / dihydropterin pyrophosphokinase (EC 2.7.6.3): ATP beta,gamma-pyrophosphoryl transfer. Two-Mg2+ transition-state stabilization is kinase chemistry, not hydrolysis. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: HPPK.

mechanism_text_snippets: The catalysis follows an inline displacement mechanism. The hydroxyl group of HP performs a nucleophilic attack to the beta-phosphate group of ATP to yield HP-pyrophosphate and AMP. Arg92, Arg82 and two Mg(II) ions stabilise the transition state. Both Mg(II) ions activate the beta-phosphorus for...

current_label.rationale: Receptor protein-tyrosine kinase performs ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer rather than metal-activated hydrolysis in the current seed definitions.

decision.rationale: receptor protein-tyrosine kinase (EC 2.7.10.x): ePK-fold Tyr protein kinase. ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer to tyrosine. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: ePK (RTK subclass).

mechanism_text_snippets: The entry represents the proposed dissociative (S<sub>N</sub>1-type) mechanism. Here a metaphosphate anion is eliminated from the ATP substrate in an intramolecular elimination. This extremely reactive intermediate then attacks the protein tyrosine substrate in an addition reaction with concomitt...

current_label.rationale: Mitogen-activated protein kinase kinase performs ATP-dependent protein phosphoryl transfer rather than any current hydrolase, flavin, heme, PLP, or cobalamin seed chemistry.

decision.rationale: MAP2K / MAPKK (EC 2.7.12.2): dual-specificity ePK-fold kinase. ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: ePK.

mechanism_text_snippets: The serine residue of the protein substrate attacks the terminal, pentavalent phosphorous of ATP. Asp194 abstracts a proton from the phospho-serine residue. Asp194 relays the proton to ADP, regenerating the active site.

current_label.rationale: Pantetheine-phosphate adenylyltransferase performs ATP-dependent nucleotidyl transfer rather than metal-activated hydrolysis or current cofactor-redox seed chemistry.

decision.rationale: pantetheine-phosphate adenylyltransferase / CoaD (EC 2.7.7.3): ATP alpha-nucleotidyl transfer producing dephospho-CoA + PPi. True nucleotidyltransferase, not a hydrolase. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: HUP / nucleotidyltransferase.

mechanism_text_snippets: One of the 4'-phosphate oxygen's of Ppant undergoes nucleophilic attack on the alpha-phosphate group of ATP with pyrophosphate as the leaving group. The pentacovalent transition state is stabilised by His18 while the leaving group is stabilised by Arg91, Ser129 and the magnesium ion.

decision.rationale: D-alanine-(R)-lactate ligase (EC 6.3.2.-): ATP-grasp ligase via phospho-intermediate then ester bond. Two-step kinase-then-ligation chemistry. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: ATP-grasp ligase.

mechanism_text_snippets: The D-lactate substrate attacks the terminal phosphate of ATP in a concerted mechanism to form ADP and phosphorylated D-lactate. current_label.rationale: Phosphofructokinase I performs ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer rather than metal-activated hydrolysis in the current seed definitions.

decision.rationale: phosphofructokinase I (EC 2.7.1.11): PfkA-fold sugar kinase; ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer to fructose-6-P. Pocket Mg2+ is ATP cofactor. Out_of_scope confirmed; future fingerprint family: PfkA.

mechanism_text_snippets: Asp128 is proposed to act as a base, abstracting a proton from 1-OH of 6-phosphofructose. This increases the nucleophilicity of the functional group which then attacks at the gamma phosphate of ATP in an SN2 reaction, forming 1,6-bisphosphate fructose and ADP.
